summary: Matched and partially decompiled a large slice of src/melee/mn/mn_2295.c, adding menu data tables, concrete function bodies, refined prototypes, and symbol-table splits/typing for related .data/.sdata. The decomp.dev bot reported 19 new GALE01 matches and 11 improved unmatched functions, raising matched code by 2692 bytes (+0.07%).
what_changed: Replaced many mn_2295.c nonmatching stubs with C implementations, including functions reported as newly matched such as fn_80229BF4, mn_8022BEDC, fn_8022AF10, mn_8022BE34, fn_8022BDB4, mn_8022F360, mn_8022BCF8, mn_8022C010, mn_8022F218, mn_8022F3D8, mn_8022F268, mn_8022BD8C, fn_8022BCD4, mn_8022EAE0, mn_80229DC0, mn_8022BD6C, mn_8022EB04, mn_8022EA08, and mn_8022ED6C.

Added large static menu/animation data in mn_2295.c, including u16 tables, Vec3 animation frame tables, and a mn_803EB6B0 table containing Vec3 pointers, offsets, text/id tables, counts, and function pointers.

Improved several still-unmatched mn_2295 functions according to the bot report, including mn_8022A5D0, mn_80229F60, mn_8022B3A0, fn_8022AFEC, mn_8022C4F4, mn_8022BA1C, mn_8022A440, mn_8022EFD8, mn_8022ADD8, mn_8022F138, and mn_8022F1A8.
Expanded mn_2295.h prototypes from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to typed signatures for many mn_2295 functions, and annotated mn_unk1.x0/x1 as current/previous menu while changing x2 from s16 to u16.
Updated lb_00F9.h to give lb_8001204C a concrete signature: void lb_8001204C(int, HSD_JObj**, u16*, int).
Added ordinal comments to MajorSceneKind values in gm/types.h from /* 00 */ MJ_TITLE through /* 2D */ MJ_COUNT, preserving the enum values but making numeric scene IDs explicit.

Refined config/GALE01/symbols.txt around mn_2295 data by splitting a previously broad mn_803EAE68 object into many smaller typed data symbols, marking float and 2-byte data correctly, resizing mn_804D4B80 to a 1-byte object, and renaming several local string objects from mn_* to lbl_*.
smart_moves: Used data-symbol refinement as part of matching: the diff split broad .data ranges into accurately sized Vec3/float/u16 objects, which made C static initializers line up with linker expectations.
Gave lb_8001204C a real prototype before using it in mn_2295.c; this made repeated jobj-array extraction calls expressible without relying on UNK_PARAMS.
Encoded MajorSceneKind ordinal comments in the enum rather than changing enum values, improving readability for menu/state code that uses numeric IDs without risking ABI changes.
Modeled menu animation ranges as Vec3 and u16 lookup tables, allowing code to call HSD_JObjReqAnimAll, HSD_JObjAnimAll, and helper functions against named data instead of opaque raw addresses.
Introduced typed function-pointer table entries in mn_803EB6B0 for menu-specific handlers, making dispatch structure visible and reusable for future matching work in this file.
decomp_lessons: When a menu file is dominated by animation frame constants, first recover the data tables and symbol sizes; correct float/u16/string typing can unlock multiple function matches at once.

Replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS with precise prototypes in headers can be a matching tactic, not just cleanup, because calls then compile with the same argument passing and type conversions as the target.
For enum-heavy game-mode code, inline ordinal comments such as /* 1A */ MJ_DEBUG_GOVER are useful evidence for numeric scene IDs while keeping the decompiled C semantically unchanged.

Small utility functions can close easy matches once surrounding types are known; this PR matched simple routines that iterate GObj entity lists, test gm_801A36A0 flags, set gmMainLib state, and stop FObj animations.
String symbols should not be left as broad mn_* data objects when they are local labels; resizing/renaming them to lbl_* with exact string sizes improves map fidelity.
naming_conventions: Kept address-based names for still-unknown mn data symbols, e.g. mn_803EAE8C, mn_803EB6B0, mn_804D4B30.
Used fn_8022.... names for local/static or callback-like functions where project naming has not assigned a module prefix name, e.g. fn_80229BF4 and fn_8022AF10.
Renamed local string symbols in the map from mn_803EB958-style names to lbl_803EB958-style labels.
Documented struct fields conservatively with offset-style names such as x0, x1, x2, x4, x8, xC, x10, only adding comments where evidence was clear: current menu and previous menu.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK in implemented functions such as mn_8022F138/mn_8022F1A8 and other mn_2295 bodies where stack layout needed preservation.
Added a small dontinline wrapper, mn_80229A04_dontinline, around mn_80229A04, indicating an inlining/codegen control tactic for matching.
Used concrete HSD animation APIs and object traversal patterns: HSD_JObjReqAnimAll, HSD_JObjAnimAll, HSD_JObjReqAnim, HSD_ForeachAnim, HSD_FObjStopAnim, HSD_GObjPLink_80390228, and HSD_GObj_80390CD4.
Used typed data declarations with exact array dimensions, such as Vec3 mn_803EAE8C[0x22][3] and u16 text/id arrays, to reproduce .data layout.
Matched animation helper mn_8022F3D8 by forwarding HSD_ForeachAnim with JOBJ_TYPE, a supplied HSD_TypeMask, callback mn_8022F360, AOBJ_ARG_AOU, and an object-type argument.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the slice.
decomp.dev bot reported GALE01 matched code improved to 35.33%, with +2692 bytes and 19 new matches plus 11 improved unmatched functions.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that finish remaining mn_2295 functions after mn_8022F410.
Search for uses of lb_8001204C signature changes in other menu files.
Search for later renames or struct refinement of mn_803EB6B0_t and mn_unk1.
Compare other PRs that add MajorSceneKind ordinal comments or use scene ID comments for matching.
